concept.description: Taco Bell Express — smaller-format quick-service Mexican food units in non-traditional locations

fees.royalty_rate_traditional: 5.5% (for comparison — traditional franchises under separate FDD)
fees.successor_fee: Greater of $11,250 or 1/2 current initial fee
fees.liquidated_damages: Greater of 11% of last 12 months Gross Sales or $100,000

territory.description: Non-exclusive license for single specified location. No territorial protection.

term_and_renewal.successor_policy: Discretionary, subject to modification/cancellation at any time

noncompete.during_term: No Mexican-style food restaurant interest (except ≤10% publicly traded)
noncompete.post_term: 1 year, 10-mile radius, only if terminated for breach
noncompete.relationship_agreement: Broader — no QSR, Mexican casual/quick casual during term + 1 year post

state_overrides.Illinois: IL law governs; IL forum required
state_overrides.Minnesota: Cannot require litigation outside MN; liquidated damages void
state_overrides.California: 180 days non-renewal notice; CA Civil Code §1671 limits liquidated damages
state_overrides.Washington: WA FIPA applies; attorneys fees only if franchisor prevails

state_addenda.material_overrides_summary: MN: liquidated damages void, 90-day termination notice, litigation must be in MN. IL: IL law governs, IL forum required. CA: 180-day non-renewal notice, liquidated damages may be unenforceable. NY: GBL Article 33 rights preserved, licensee may terminate on any legal grounds.

red_flags: No FPR — zero financial performance disclosure for Express Units
System is shrinking — 27 net licensed unit losses in 3 years
10% royalty rate (nearly double the 5.5% Traditional rate) with no territorial protection
No renewal rights — successor entirely discretionary and revocable
Liquidated damages on voluntary termination ($100K minimum)
Franchise incentives growing ($34M→$43M) — may indicate unit economics pressure
Securitization structure adds complexity and limits franchisor flexibility
$937.5M in securitization notes due May 2026 — refinancing risk

unresolveds.description: No FPR — zero financial performance disclosure for Express Units. Prospective licensees have no official guidance on revenues, costs, or profitability.
Franchise incentives growing ($34M→$43M). Are Express units specifically receiving incentives? What are the terms?
Revenue breakdown between Traditional ($663M total) and Express not disclosed. Express represents ~3.6% of system units.
Integrated Expansion Policy content not in FDD body. Referenced as source of possible temporary impact protection.
7 company-owned units reacquired in Texas in 2024 — reason for licensee exit not disclosed.
2025 follow-on securitization planned but not yet executed. May affect fee structures or covenant requirements.
unresolveds.source: Item 1, PDF page 8; Exhibit G Note 6, PDF page 138

contradictions.description: Royalty rate differential: Express 10% vs Traditional 5.5%. Not a contradiction per se but creates significant cost disadvantage for Express format. Not prominently flagged in FDD body.
contradictions.resolution_status: Confirmed consistent — differential is by design

contradictions.description: System trajectory vs projected growth: Item 20 shows declining system (-27 over 3 years) but projects 8 new units. Projections may be optimistic given 11-20 closures per year.
contradictions.resolution_status: Not contradictory — openings and closures coexist — but pattern warrants caution

contradictions.description: No renewal rights vs elaborate successor policy: Item 17 states 'No renewal rights' but describes successor policy in detail. Legally distinct but potentially confusing.

contract_provisions.modernization_protection: No re-imaging required unless Company implements in ≥25% of company-operated US units (LA §5.1)
contract_provisions.strict_conformity_clause: Any failure to adhere to standards = material breach (LA §3.4)
contract_provisions.voluntary_termination_penalty: Treated as default — triggers liquidated damages (LA §15.5)

supplier_control_enriched.aggregator_royalties_2024: $46.5M to TBC from DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub
